front: What is a graph in Graph Theory?
back: A graph G = (V, E) is a mathematical structure consisting of a set of vertices V (also called nodes) and a set of edges E that connect pairs of vertices.

front: Define vertex and edge in a graph.
back: A vertex (or node) is a fundamental unit of a graph. An edge (or arc) is a connection between two vertices. If edge connects vertices u and v, it is denoted as (u,v).

front: What is the difference between a simple graph and a multigraph?
back: A simple graph has at most one edge between any pair of vertices and no self-loops. A multigraph allows multiple edges between the same pair of vertices (parallel edges) but typically no self-loops.
A pseudograph is a graph that allows both multiple edges between vertices (parallel edges) and self-loops (edges connecting a vertex to itself).
A directed graph (digraph) is a graph where edges have a direction, going from one vertex to another. An edge is represented as an ordered pair (u,v), indicating direction from u to v.
A weighted graph is a graph where each edge has an associated numerical value called weight (or cost), representing quantities like distance, time, or capacity.

front: Define the degree of a vertex.
back: The degree of a vertex is the number of edges incident to it. In directed graphs, we have in-degree (incoming edges) and out-degree (outgoing edges).
A pendant vertex (or leaf vertex) is a vertex with degree 1, meaning it is connected to exactly one other vertex in the graph.
An isolated vertex is a vertex with degree 0, meaning it has no edges connecting it to any other vertex in the graph.
The sum of degrees of all vertices in an undirected graph equals twice the number of edges: Σdeg(v) = 2|E|. This implies the number of odd-degree vertices must be even.
A complete graph Kn is a simple graph with n vertices where every pair of distinct vertices is connected by a unique edge. It has n(n-1)/2 edges.
A cycle graph Cn is a graph with n vertices (n≥3) arranged in a closed chain where each vertex is connected to exactly two others, forming a simple cycle.
A path graph Pn is a graph with n vertices that can be arranged in a linear sequence where each vertex (except endpoints) is connected to exactly two others.
A subgraph is a graph whose vertex set is a subset of the original graph's vertex set, and whose edge set is a subset of the original graph's edge set, with edges only between vertices in the subset.

front: What is the complement of a graph?
back: The complement of a graph G, denoted G̅, is a graph on the same vertex set where two vertices are adjacent if and only if they were NOT adjacent in G.
